title: Two-phase validation with collected ops before any write: defend this design
description: The Phase 1 / Phase 2 split in contact_set and space_set is the correct architecture for RFC 8620 §7.1 atomicity. A future contributor might suggest simplifying to 'validate and write as you go' to reduce code. That would reintroduce the pre-fix bug: blocked=true writes before displayName:42 fails its type check, leaving a partial patch committed. The correct invariant is: no store call happens until all per-patch validation passes. Tests nie-uf6a.1 (test_contact_set_blocked_and_bad_displayname_type_is_atomic) and the mixed-known-unknown-keys test are the regression guards for this.
